prose:
Method–provenance summary (fukui reactivity of aniline)

• Property: condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−, dual descriptor) and most reactive atomic sites. Headline value reported here: maximum nucleophilic Fukui index f₋.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`), no density-fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x backend.
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; single-point property evaluation (no re-optimization).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 for the neutral state; N–1 and N+1 states treated as doublets (default for a closed-shell parent) to obtain f– and f+.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`, PySCF backend for DFT).
• Convergence & integrity: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states. Integrity checks report status “ok” and trustworthy = true. No `warnings` array present; integrity checks flagged only informational warnings:
  – fukui_charge_conservation_f+: "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – fukui_charge_conservation_f-: "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – schema_header: "common header present"
  (All checks marked ok.)
• Live log: the SCF and population-analysis output was streamed in real time to the automatically generated .out file announced at launch (path printed on start; e.g. `fukui_20260706-173112.out`). The summarized JSON result was written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpr471y4ss/live.json.

Result (headline)
The most nucleophilic atom is N (atom 1), with Fukui f₋ = 0.20452359 (dimensionless).

No additional warnings were reported by the engine.

This value is a computed result (DFT single-point on the supplied geometry) and should be interpreted within the known accuracy regime of semi-local DFT partial-charge–derived Fukui indices.
